english_text:
Subject
Experience
To bring to one's knees
To completely incapacitate
To overpower
To render incapable of response
alt_text: Etymology diagram of the Arabic root '-j-z. Three-step vertical chain: مُعجزۃ (Subject — miracle) → أَعجاز (Experience — incapacitation) → عَجز (the bare root), with English glosses listing four shades of meaning.
notes: Pedagogical Arabic morphology — the noun 'mu'jiza' (miracle) literally means 'that which incapacitates'.
